title: Cardinality Principle (Last Number Said)
description: When asked "how many" after counting a set, the student states the last number they said as the total quantity.
condition: After counting a set of objects and being asked "How many are there?"
response: State the last number spoken in the count as the total number of objects.
label: Rule / Plan
example: A student counts cubes: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. When asked, "How many cubes are there?" they respond, "5."
notes: Core K.CC.B.4 understanding that the last number name tells the number of objects counted.

title: Next Number in Count Sequence
description: Given a number in the counting sequence, identify the number that comes immediately after it.
condition: Given a spoken number in the standard counting sequence and asked what number comes next.
response: State the next number in the counting sequence.
label: Rule / Plan
example: If asked, "What number comes after 7?" the student responds, "8."
notes: Relies on memorized verbal count sequence (K.CC.A.1).

title: One-to-One Correspondence in Counting
description: While counting objects, pair each object with exactly one number word and each number word with exactly one object.
condition: Given a set of objects to count.
response: Touch or move each object once while saying one number word for each object.
label: Production / Skill
example: For 5 cubes, the student points to each cube once while saying, "1, 2, 3, 4, 5," without skipping or double-counting.
notes: Includes keeping track of which objects have been counted.

title: Recount After Quantity Changes
description: When objects are added to or removed from a set, recount to determine the new total.
condition: Given a previously counted set where objects have been added or removed.
response: Recount the entire set using one-to-one correspondence and state the new total.
label: Rule / Plan
example: A student counts 5 cubes. Two cubes are removed. The student recounts: 1, 2, 3, and says, "3 cubes."
notes: Assesses flexibility of counting and reapplication of counting procedure.

title: Stable Order of Count Sequence
description: Recite number words in the conventional fixed order when counting.
condition: Asked to count aloud from 1 upward.
response: Say the number words in the standard order (e.g., 1, 2, 3, ...).
label: Rule / Plan
